notes:
RESEARCH (sourced, adversarial):
- Set structure (Bulbapedia Flashfire_(TCG), fetched OK): English Flashfire (XY2) released 2014-05-07, 109 cards = 106 base + 3 secret rares (#107-109, all Mega-EX: M Charizard EX x2, M Kangaskhan EX). Repo snapshot card data (data/snapshot/snapshot.json, flashfire set) gives EXACT rarity counts matching the given buckets: common 31, uncommon 33, rare 20, rare-holo 8, ultra-rare 15, secret-rare 3.
- ultra-rare bucket (15) breaks down as: 5 regular Pokemon-EX (#11/12 Charizard, #35 Magnezone, #41 Toxicroak, #78 Kangaskhan = "Rare Holo EX"), 2 Mega-EX (#13 M Charizard, #79 M Kangaskhan), 4 Full Art EX (#100-103), 3 Full Art Trainers (#104 Lysandre, #105 Pokemon Center Lady, #106 Pokemon Fan Club). The set's bucket scheme lumps ALL of these into one "ultra-rare" id, so the regular-EX rate and the UR/Full-Art rate are SUMMED into the single ultra-rare bucket.

- Pull rates (Flipside Gaming 'Comprehensive Review of Rarity Part Two', fetched OK): EX rates only "dropped to ~1:6 packs by Ancient Origins"; EARLIER XY sets (Flashfire is the 2nd XY set, May 2014) "matched the Plasma Blast period" = ~1:18 packs for regular EX and ~1:36 packs for Ultra Rares (Mega-EX/Full Art). Secret Rares "retained their 1:72 pack pull rate" through the XY era. These three anchors are the hard published figures, applied as ERA rates (no large per-set Flashfire opening study exists).
- Community corroboration (Amazon Flashfire booster-box reviews via WebSearch; PokeBeach pull-rate thread, page 403'd but search snippet usable): box openings report ~3-6 EX-class hits/box and secret rares appearing roughly once per 1-2 boxes — consistent with the era anchors below. PokeBeach snippet: boxes range 3-7 ultra rares (low end = 3 regular EX, high end includes a secret + 2 full arts), confirming high variance around the ~3-3.5 EX-class/box mean.

- Pack structure (Bulbapedia Booster_pack_(TCG), fetched OK): D&P-onward English boosters = 10 cards; the extra Basic Energy 11th card only began in Sun & Moon, so XY packs = 10 game cards. XY-era baseline: 4 common + 3 uncommon + 1 reverse-holo + 1 rare-or-better (= 9 modeled; the 10th is an unmodeled code/ad card, within validator's 1-card tolerance).
- Products (Bulbapedia Elite_Trainer_Box_(TCG), fetched OK): EXPLICITLY states "Plasma Freeze, Legendary Treasures, and Flashfire did not receive an ETB" due to the staggered launch schedule. So etb = null. Standard 36-pack Booster Display Box confirmed (Amazon/ToyWiz/Bulbapedia listings). display.packs = 36.

MAPPING ONTO BUCKETS (rare-or-better slot, count=1, per-pack):
  ultra-rare = regular-EX(1/18=0.05556) + UR/FullArt(1/36=0.02778) = 0.08333
  secret-rare = 1/72 = 0.01389  (1 per 2 boxes)
  rare-holo = derived ~8.5 plain holo rares per 36 packs (after EX/FA/secret carve-outs) = 0.23611/pack — RECONSTRUCTED from era box structure, not a per-set study (hence confidence=low).
  rare (non-holo) = remainder = 1 - 0.08333 - 0.01389 - 0.23611 = 0.66667
  Sum check: 0.66667 + 0.23611 + 0.08333 + 0.01389 = 1.00000 — exact.

HIT ECONOMY SANITY: any-EX-or-better ~9.7%/pack (~3.5/box: ~2 regular EX + ~1 Full-Art/Mega + ~0.5 secret), secret-rare ~1.4% (1 per 2 boxes). Matches Flipside era anchors + community box reports; not over-claimed. Vintage sealed Flashfire box has a large collector premium (M Charizard EX chase), so EV/booster sits far below the sealed box price — expected for a 2014 set, rates NOT inflated to compensate.

CONFIDENCE = low: the EX/UR/secret rates are published ERA figures (Plasma Blast-equivalent early-XY) applied without a per-set Flashfire opening study; the rare-holo vs non-holo split is reconstructed from era box structure.
